results.possibleIdentity: Denis W. Trover is an American engineer and physicist who was active in the technical and artistic communities during the late 1960s and 1970s. He graduated from Michigan State University in 1967 with a Bachelor of Science in Physics. During his university years, he was deeply involved in photography and journalism, serving as the Photography Director and Business Manager for the MSU underground student newspaper, "The Paper." His professional career led him to International Business Machines (IBM), where he worked as an engineer in technical fields including optics, holography, and lasers. He was based in Wappingers Falls, New York, a location closely associated with IBM's East Fishkill and Poughkeepsie facilities. Trover was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that fostered collaborations between the technical and artistic sectors. His technical contributions also extended to publishing; he is credited as a technical illustrator for technical books such as "Welding Projects: A Design Approach" (1976) and was active in early handheld electronics communities, such as the HP-65 Users Club in the late 1970s.
